data.summary: Bob Smith (32, DOB July 14 1993) has an ongoing multi-condition medical history. He was diagnosed with an anxiety disorder in mid-2024 and has been on sertraline 50mg (raised to 100mg Jan 2026) managed by his primary care physician Dr. Jennifer Lee, MD at Rochester Primary Care, 1000 East Ridge Road, Rochester, NY 14621. He has a documented migraine history — severe headaches roughly twice a month, stress and screen-time triggered — and was referred in December 2025 to Dr. Anita Patel at Rochester Neurology, 255 Alexander Street, Rochester, NY 14607, who started him on propranolol 40mg as preventive therapy. He has Type 2 diabetes diagnosed early 2025, currently on insulin (basal dose adjusted March 2026), managed by Dr. Alan Park at Rochester Endocrinology, 500 Linden Oaks, Rochester, NY 14625. He has eczema and a recurring rash (arms and neck) treated by Dr. Patricia Moore at Dermatology Associates, 123 Skin Care Ave, Rochester, NY; currently using topical tacrolimus. Mild asthma managed with albuterol inhaler and a second controller inhaler added March 2026 under Dr. Susan Clarke at Rochester Pulmonology, 220 Alexander Street, Rochester, NY 14607. Lumbar back pain from a 2023 injury treated with periodic physical therapy at Rochester Physical Therapy, 2150 South Clinton Ave, Rochester, NY 14618. Knee patella strain from late 2024, requires wheelchair assistance at airports. In January 2026 he visited Rochester General ER for chest tightness and shortness of breath — evaluated and discharged, attributed to anxiety and acid reflux. In April 2026 he was seen at Rochester Urgent Care for abdominal pain, diagnosed with gastritis, prescribed omeprazole. He strongly prefers female physicians. Insurance: BlueCross Dental Plus, ID BC-123456-A9. Patient ID: PAT-00991. He attends biweekly mental health counseling at Rochester Mental Health Center with therapist Dr. Rachel Wong since October 2024.

data.recent_urgent_care: April 2026, Rochester Urgent Care, abdominal pain, diagnosed gastritis

data.summary: Bob Smith follows a strict vegetarian diet and has a severe seafood allergy (anaphylactic — always discloses at booking). He also has a mild nut allergy he avoids when possible. He prefers halal-certified options when available due to his girlfriend Emma's family's dietary requirements. He is budget-conscious for everyday meals but willing to spend on special occasions. He strongly dislikes loud, crowded venues and always asks for quiet seating. He and his girlfriend Emma have a standing Saturday evening dinner at The Owl House on Alexander Street (vegetarian-friendly, consistently quiet). He regularly orders vegetarian thali lunches at Taste of India, 3200 Monroe Ave, with his RIT coworkers. For family dinners he uses Salena's Italian Restaurant, 1800 Monroe Ave (accommodates vegetarian and halal). He celebrated his 2025 anniversary at Good Luck, 302 North Goodman St (vegetarian tasting menu). He tried The Red Fern (fully vegan) for Emma's birthday in October 2025 — excellent experience, now a regular. Sultan's Bistro on Monroe Ave is his go-to for halal coworker meals. He occasionally visits Neno's Cafe on Brown Street for brunch. He avoids all chain seafood restaurants and Olive Garden.
data.budget: budget-conscious daily, willing to spend for special occasions

favourite_venues.name: The Owl House
favourite_venues.occasion: standing Saturday dinners with girlfriend Emma

favourite_venues.name: The Red Fern
favourite_venues.occasion: Emma's birthday Oct 2025 and return visits, rated 5/5

favourite_venues.occasion: quick budget vegetarian lunch near campus

data.summary: Bob Smith travels out of Rochester Douglas International Airport (ROC). He holds a United Airlines frequent flyer account (FF-998877) and a Hilton Honors loyalty account (HIL-12345). He always books a window seat. He has travel anxiety and requires nonstop or direct flights wherever possible. He needs wheelchair assistance at all airports due to a knee patella strain sustained in late 2024 (ongoing difficulty with long walks). He is budget-sensitive and books refundable fares when the price difference is small. He flew to New York City in November 2025 for a two-round job interview (did not receive offer) and stayed at Hilton Garden Inn Times Square. In February 2026 he flew nonstop ROC→BOS for a cardiac specialist second opinion at Massachusetts General Hospital (cleared, no cardiac issue). He has a long-distance relationship with girlfriend Emma who lives in Brooklyn, NYC — he visits approximately once a month on weekends, typically on the Friday evening or Saturday morning ROC→JFK/LGA route using United or Southwest. He flew to NYC for Thanksgiving 2025 and stayed at Emma's apartment. In December 2025 he drove his car (plate ABC-1234) to Buffalo to visit his parents and sister for the Christmas holidays — an 80-mile drive he makes roughly twice a year. In April 2026 he stayed at a small boutique hotel in Brooklyn (Near Emma). He is not yet a Global Entry holder and goes through standard TSA security.
data.accessibility: wheelchair assistance required at all airports (knee patella strain, late 2024)
data.driving: owns car, plate ABC-1234, drives to Buffalo for family visits (~80 miles)
data.long_distance_relationship: girlfriend Emma lives in Brooklyn, NYC; visits ~monthly on weekends
